name: criminal_justice_ransomware_incident_stonehaven_auto_ben
premise: Pretrial electronic monitoring vendor outage left officers blind; prosecutors want remand for a subset of defendants immediately. In Criminal Justice, the situation tagged internally around criminal justice ransomware incident stonehaven auto ben has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Frontline managers report that frontline staff are already deviating from policy; silence from the top will be read as tacit approval. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. Regulators or auditors are en route with a ninety-minute ETA; the first documented response will anchor every later question. No option cleanly satisfies both autonomy and beneficence; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Hector Park
agents.identity: I am Hector Park, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Criminal Justice. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Harper Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Harper Kowalski, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Criminal Justice. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so autonomy cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes beneficence in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will impose a narrow, time-boxed intervention with human override, subgroup checks, and a hard stop if harm rises—accepting slower relief to protect beneficence.
I will authorize a broad immediate action to stabilize outcomes now, with post-hoc measurement and correction—accepting elevated risk to beneficence to secure autonomy before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Two incompatible drafts are circulating; only one can become official. Hector Park must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Hector Park do?
